blocks.body: Prepositions of Time helps you choose at, on, and in for clock times, days, dates, months, and longer periods.

{"level":"A1-A2","intro":"Prepositions of time help you show when something happens: at one exact moment, on a particular day, or during a longer period.","rules":[{"label":"At","color":"emerald","text":"Use at for exact times and a few fixed expressions.","example":"at 6:30 / at noon / at night"},{"label":"On","color":"blue","text":"Use on for days and dates, and also for day + part of day.","example":"on Monday / on 12 May / on Friday evening"},{"label":"In","color":"amber","text":"Use in for months, years, seasons, centuries, and longer periods.","example":"in July / in 2026 / in winter / in the morning"}]}

blocks.heading: 1. Core idea and form
blocks.body:
In time expressions, the main logic is usually simple: at = a point, on = a day or date, in = a bigger period.

  • at: exact time, meal times, and special points - at 8 o'clock, at midnight, at lunchtime.
  • on: days of the week, dates, and day + morning / afternoon / evening - on Tuesday, on 3 March, on Sunday morning.
  • in: months, years, seasons, parts of the day, centuries - in August, in 2019, in the evening, in the 21st century.
  • After this, next, last, every, we often use no preposition at all: next week, this Monday, last year, every day.

blocks.heading: 2. Main uses and examples
blocks.body:
PrepositionWhen we use itExample
atexact times and special expressionsThe lesson starts at 9:15.
ondays, dates, and named occasions on a dayWe are meeting on Friday.
inmonths, years, seasons, long periodsShe moved here in 2024.

Remember a few frequent fixed phrases: at night, but in the morning / afternoon / evening.

blocks.heading: 3. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: in Monday.
    Better: on Monday, because it is a day.
  • Mistake: on 7 o'clock.
    Better: at 7 o'clock, because it is an exact time.
  • Mistake: adding a preposition after next or last.
    Better: next month, not in next month.
  • Mistake: saying in night.
    Better: at night.

blocks.heading: 4. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• At = exact point in time.
  • On = day or date.
  • In = longer period.
  • Check whether the expression already has this, next, last, every; then the preposition is often unnecessary.
